place: Cherry Hill Mall CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Cherry Hill got its name from the cherry trees that once lined the original farm property, later becoming the Cherry Hill Inn and today a movie theater complex.
Croft Farm, built in 1753 as a working mill, still stands as a historic landmark in Cherry Hill.
The Cherry Hill Public Library, covering 72,000 square feet, ranks among New Jersey’s largest municipal libraries.
NJ Transit’s Atlantic City Line stops at Cherry Hill station, situated between the Garden State Pavilion and the former racetrack grounds.
The Cherry Hill Historical Commission preserves the town’s heritage, collecting archives and memorabilia in the local history room of the public library.
King's Christian School, a private Pre‑K–12 institution, opened in 1946 as the Christian Day School of Camden County.
